description:
You are a machine-learning engineer about to start a data–preparation project.  
  Before writing any code you first want a clean, isolated Python environment that already has the two
  core data-handling libraries you always use.

  Your goal:
  1. Inside the folder “/home/user/projects/data_prep” create a Python 3 virtual environment named “ml_env”
     using the standard venv module.
  2. Activate that environment and install *exactly*
        • pandas version 1.5.3  
        • numpy  version 1.24.2
     (no other versions).
  3. Still while the environment is activated, output the full, frozen list of packages to  
     “/home/user/projects/data_prep/env_packages.log”.
     Use the normal “pip freeze” style so that each line is  
     “package_name==version_number” with no leading/trailing spaces.
     The checker will specifically look for the two required lines:
         numpy==1.24.2  
         pandas==1.5.3
     but the order does not matter and additional default lines such as
     “pip==...”, “setuptools==...”, etc. are fine.

  Summary of artefacts that must exist when you are done:
    • Directory:  /home/user/projects/data_prep/ml_env/
      (the standard content created by python ‑m venv, e.g., bin/, lib/ …).
    • File:       /home/user/projects/data_prep/env_packages.log
      – plain ASCII text  
      – at least one line that is exactly “numpy==1.24.2”  
      – at least one line that is exactly “pandas==1.5.3”

  Do everything from a normal, unprivileged shell session; no sudo is required.
  One concise terminal command sequence is enough, but use whatever interactive steps you prefer.
truth:
The automated tests will verify:

  1. Directory “/home/user/projects/data_prep/ml_env” exists
     and contains the standard sub-directory “bin”.
  2. File “/home/user/projects/data_prep/env_packages.log” exists
     and is regular (not empty).
  3. Running:
        grep -Fxq "numpy==1.24.2" /home/user/projects/data_prep/env_packages.log
        grep -Fxq "pandas==1.5.3" /home/user/projects/data_prep/env_packages.log
     must both succeed (exit code 0).
